Remember, when it comes to lipstick, always wear the color not the product.

repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library. Performing Arts Special Collections
description_tesim: Photograph of actors from the film, "Greenwich Village," (l to r): William Bendix, Don Ameche, Vivian Blaine, Adolph Green, unidentified cast member (at piano) and B. S. Pully (in background at far right).

Actor/singer/songwriter, Adolph Green, was a member of the cabaret group, The Revuers. The film, "Greenwich Village," marked the screen debut of The Revuers,  and although Hollywood Reporter indicated that The Revuers' "satiric sketch of a Shubert operetta" had been purchased by the studio for the film, their sequence was cut from the finished picture. The group appears only in the party scene at the apartment of character, 'Bonnie Watson.' In that scene, two of The Revuers, Alvin Hammer and Adolph Green, are also seen singing the song, "When You Wore a Tulip and I Wore a Big Red Rose," alongside actors, B. S. Pully, Don Ameche and William Bendix. The scene depicted in this photograph, however, does not appear in the film.
